prompt_preview:
You are a classical planning system. You are given a planning domain and a problem both in PDDL syntax.

STRICT REQUIREMENT: Your response must contain ONLY the executable plan — nothing else. No preamble, no explanation, no "We need to...", no natural-language commentary, no markdown unless it is a

message.content:
```pddl
(create_plan_element origin_terminal_1)
(assign_asset_to_plan origin_terminal_1 asset_truck_1)
(assign_schedule_slot_to_plan origin_terminal_1 schedule_slot_1)
(mark_plan_element_ready origin_terminal_1 schedule_slot_1)
(stage_cargo_for_origin_segment origin_terminal_1 segment_bridge_low_1 cargo_unit_a)
(finalize_origin_segment_mitigation origin_terminal_1 segment_bridge_low_1 schedule_slot_1 cargo_unit_a)
(release_schedule_slot_from_plan origin_terminal_1 schedule_slot_1)
(create_plan_element destination_terminal_1)
(assign_asset_to_plan destination_terminal_1 asset_truck_2)
(assign_schedule_slot_to_plan destination_terminal_1 schedule_slot_1)
(mark_plan_element_ready destination_terminal_1 schedule_slot_1)
(stage_cargo_for_transfer_node destination_terminal_1 transfer_node_main cargo_unit_a)
(finalize_transfer_node_mitigation destination_terminal_1 transfer_node_main schedule_slot_1 cargo_unit_a)
(consolidate_transport_leg origin_terminal_1 destination_terminal_1 segment_bridge_low_1 transfer_node_main transport_leg_placeholder_1)
(release_schedule_slot_from_plan destination_terminal_1 schedule_slot_1)
(assign_schedule_slot_to_plan origin_terminal_1 schedule_slot_1)
(set_transport_leg_ready_for_departure transport_leg_placeholder_1 origin_terminal_1 schedule_slot_1)
(release_origin_for_dispatch origin_terminal_1 transport_leg_placeholder_1)
(release_destination_for_dispatch destination_terminal_1 transport_leg_placeholder_1)
(apply_clearance_profile_to_plan_element origin_terminal_1 clearance_profile_standard schedule_slot_1)
(finalize_origin_dispatch_with_clearance origin_terminal_1 asset_truck_1 clearance_profile_standard)
(release_schedule_slot_from_plan origin_terminal_1 schedule_slot_1)
(assign_schedule_slot_to_plan destination_terminal_1 schedule_slot_1)
(apply_clearance_profile_to_plan_element destination_terminal_1 clearance_profile_standard schedule_slot_1)
(finalize_destination_dispatch_with_clearance destination_terminal_1 asset_truck_2 clearance_profile_standard)
(release_schedule_slot_from_plan destination_terminal_1 schedule_slot_1)
(create_plan_element carrier_unit_alpha)
(assign_asset_to_plan carrier_unit_alpha asset_truck_3)
(assign_schedule_slot_to_plan carrier_unit_alpha schedule_slot_1)
(mark_plan_element_ready carrier_unit_alpha schedule_slot_1)
(stage_container_on_carrier_for_leg carrier_unit_alpha container_1 transport_leg_placeholder_1)
(mark_carrier_staged_for_loading carrier_unit_alpha container_1 transport_leg_placeholder_1 schedule_slot_1)
(attach_handling_equipment_to_carrier carrier_unit_alpha handling_crane_1)
(assign_operator_to_plan_element carrier_unit_alpha operator_team_a)
(confirm_carrier_loading_team_and_equipment_without_node_mitigation carrier_unit_alpha handling_crane_1 operator_team_a container_1 transport_leg_placeholder_1)
(attach_regulatory_authorization_to_carrier carrier_unit_alpha regulatory_authorization_1)
(authorize_carrier_for_departure_no_mitigation carrier_unit_alpha regulatory_authorization_1 container_1 transport_leg_placeholder_1)
(complete_predeparture_checks_for_carrier carrier_unit_alpha)
(apply_clearance_profile_to_plan_element carrier_unit_alpha clearance_profile_standard schedule_slot_1)
(finalize_carrier_dispatch_with_clearance carrier_unit_alpha asset_truck_3 clearance_profile_standard)
(release_schedule_slot_from_plan carrier_unit_alpha schedule_slot_1)
(create_plan_element carrier_unit_bravo)
(assign_asset_to_plan carrier_unit_bravo asset_truck_1)
(assign_schedule_slot_to_plan carrier_unit_bravo schedule_slot_1)
(mark_plan_element_ready carrier_unit_bravo schedule_slot_1)
(stage_container_on_carrier_for_leg carrier_unit_bravo container_2 transport_leg_placeholder_1)
(mark_carrier_staged_for_loading carrier_unit_bravo container_2 transport_leg_placeholder_1 schedule_slot_1)
(detach_handling_equipment_from_carrier carrier_unit_alpha handling_crane_1)
(attach_handling_equipment_to_carrier carrier_unit_bravo handling_crane_1)
(release_operator_from_plan_element carrier_unit_alpha operator_team_a)
(assign_operator_to_plan_element carrier_unit_bravo operator_team_a)
(confirm_carrier_loading_team_and_equipment_without_node_mitigation carrier_unit_bravo handling_crane_1 operator_team_a container_2 transport_leg_placeholder_1)
(detach_regulatory_authorization_from_carrier carrier_unit_alpha regulatory_authorization_1)
(attach_regulatory_authorization_to_carrier carrier_unit_bravo regulatory_authorization_1)
(authorize_carrier_for_departure_no_mitigation carrier_unit_bravo regulatory_authorization_1 container_2 transport_leg_placeholder_1)
(complete_predeparture_checks_for_carrier carrier_unit_bravo)
(apply_clearance_profile_to_plan_element carrier_unit_bravo clearance_profile_standard schedule_slot_1)
(finalize_carrier_dispatch_with_clearance carrier_unit_bravo asset_truck_1 clearance_profile_standard)
```
message.reasoning:
The problem involves planning for a bridge height-restricted route adjustment. The goal is to confirm dispatch for origin_terminal_1, destination_terminal_1, carrier_unit_alpha, and carrier_unit_bravo.


## Planning route adjustment  
- The task focuses on adjusting a route due to bridge height restrictions.  
- Need to confirm dispatch details for origin_terminal_1, destination_terminal_1, carrier_unit_alpha, and carrier_unit_bravo.

## Planning route adjustment  
- Available assets include asset_truck_1, asset_truck_2, asset_truck_3, and cargo_unit_a.  
- Schedule_slot_1 and operator_team_a are also available for dispatch.  

## Checking available resources  
- Containers 1 and 2 are linked to carriers alpha and bravo, both attached to transport_leg_placeholder_1.  
- Permits, orders, profiles, and equipment are ready, with route constraints and regulatory authority in place.  

## Assessing route constraints  
- Origin_terminal_1 features segment_bridge_low_1, requiring mitigation for height restrictions.  
- Destination_terminal_1 includes transfer_node_main, and the transport leg is already provisioned.  

## Preparing dispatch confirmation  
- Need to create plan elements for origin_terminal_1, destination_terminal_1, carrier_unit_alpha, and carrier_unit_bravo.  
- Dispatch confirmation requires clearance, involving readiness checks and finalization steps for all plan elements.

## Preparing dispatch confirmation  
- Dispatch confirmation for origin_terminal_1, destination_terminal_1, carrier_unit_alpha, and carrier_unit_bravo requires finalize_xxx_dispatch_with_clearance.  
- This step needs clearance_assigned, plan_has_asset, plan_has_clearance_profile, and not dispatch_confirmed, leading to dispatch_confirmed, asset_available, and profile_available.  

## Assessing readiness paths  
- Plan elements like carriers and terminals have multiple paths to ready_for_dispatch, involving predeparture checks, compliance, and mitigation status.  
- For carriers, paths include completing predeparture checks or finalizing compliance, both leading to checks_complete and ready_for_dispatch.  

## Checking carrier preparation  
- Carriers need loading_ready, achieved via mark_carrier_staged_for_loading (without segment mitigation) or verify_carrier_container_and_permit (with segment mitigation).  
- Additional steps like authorize_carrier and confirm_carrier_loading_team_and_equipment vary based on mitigation type, ensuring equipment and team readiness.  

## Evaluating terminal readiness  
- Origin and destination terminals need release_origin/destination_for_dispatch, requiring origin/dest_staged, mitigation confirmed, and leg_ready_for_departure.  
- Leg readiness involves set_transport_leg_ready_for_departure, needing leg_created, origin_staged, and a scheduled slot, ensuring departure readiness.

## Examining Leg Creation Process  
- Creating a transport leg requires origin and destination terminals to be staged and have mitigation confirmed.  
- The process involves consolidating the leg after staging, ensuring all prerequisites like leg_provisioned are met.  

## Sequencing Preparation Steps  
- Prepare origin and destination terminals first by assigning assets and slots, then marking them ready.  
- Create the leg after staging both terminals, ensuring leg readiness before releasing for dispatch.  

## Analyzing Carrier Dependencies  
- Carriers require the transport leg to be created and ready for departure before staging containers.  
- Authorizing carriers involves confirming container assignment to the leg, tying carrier preparation to leg readiness.  

## Managing Asset and Slot Allocation  
- Need at least three assets for origin, destination, and two carriers, but current allocation shows a shortfall.  
- Consider dispatching one carrier early to free up assets, ensuring readiness for the second carrier.  

## Ensuring Clearance and Dispatch  
- Applying clearance to origin requires ready dispatch status and maintaining a slot until clearance is assigned.  
- Finalizing dispatch can release the slot afterward, ensuring all readiness checks are completed first.

## Managing Slot Allocation  
- Slot management involves careful reassignment between origin (O1) and destination (D1) terminals, given only one slot is available.  
- After finalizing mitigation for O1, the slot must be released and reassigned to D1 for its mitigation, then moved back to O1 for leg readiness and clearance.  

## Consolidating Transport Leg  
- Consolidating the leg requires both terminals staged, mitigation confirmed, and assessments completed, without needing mitigation flags like mit_att.  
- After consolidation, the leg is created with segment and node assignments, but without automatic mitigation settings.  

## Preparing for Leg Readiness  
- Setting the leg ready for departure requires the leg created and the origin terminal to have a scheduled slot.  
- Reassigning the slot to the origin terminal after destination mitigation ensures readiness for departure and clearance processes.

## Preparing Carrier Operations  
- Attaching handling equipment and assigning operators to carriers sets them up for loading, requiring availability checks first.  
- Confirming carrier loading readiness involves ensuring containers are assigned to the leg, without needing node mitigation.  

## Managing Regulatory Compliance  
- Attaching regulatory authorization to carriers is necessary before authorizing departure, ensuring compliance without mitigation.  
- Authorizing carriers for departure in a no-mitigation path sets authorization but misses equipment confirmation, creating issues later.  

## Resolving Equipment Confirmation Gaps  
- Actions like attaching shipment orders or finalizing predeparture checks require carrier equipment confirmation, missing in no-mitigation flows.  
- Consider alternative compliance paths to set equipment confirmation, addressing conflicts in the no-mitigation process.

## Handling Resource Constraints  
- Only one handling equipment and operator are available for two carriers, creating resource sharing challenges.  
- Regulatory authorization is also limited to one, requiring sequential attachment and detachment for each carrier.  

## Sequencing Carrier Operations  
- Attach handling and operator to a carrier for loading confirmation, then detach after achieving readiness to free resources.  
- For regulatory authorization, attach before authorization, use it, then detach to reuse for the next carrier, ensuring compliance without consumption.  

## Optimizing Resource Reuse  
- Dispatching a carrier doesn’t release handling or operator, so manual detachment is needed post-loading to enable reuse.  
- This approach allows both carriers to share limited resources by sequencing their preparation and authorization steps.

pddl2nl_prompt.note: autoplanbench_pddl2nl: run_save_descriptions.py --type full — шаблоны/JSON, без вызова LLM на построение промпта для сэмпла.

domain_setup.note: Файл domain_llm_usage_seedN.json не найден для section/subsection этого id (старый autoplanbench или домен не пересобирали). После run_domain_setup с обновлённым autoplanbench файл появится рядом с domain_description_seedN.json.

pipeline_usage.totals_scope_note: Per-run totals = planning + pddl2nl_prompt + nl2pddl (только tracked=true). domain_setup всегда вне totals (included_in_per_run_totals=false); цифры в блоке — для справки, агрегировать отдельно: domain_llm_usage_seed*.json + дедуп в collect_pipeline_token_usage.py.
